title: Peruvian Cannellini Bean Empanadas
description: Tangy, crumbly, indulgent empanadas filled with cannellini beans, inspired by Peruvian cuisine, suitable for a macrobiotic-friendly diet.

ingredients.name: onion, diced
ingredients.notes: cooking time reduced to prevent burning

ingredients.name: tomato, diced
ingredients.notes: fresh or canned, whichever is readily available

ingredients.name: cumin, ground
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste for optimal flavor

instructions: Preheat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
In a separate bowl, mash the cooked cannellini beans using a fork or a potato masher until mostly smooth.
Assemble the dough according to package instructions and roll it out to about 1/8 inch thickness.
Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and begin rolling out small portions of dough to form circles, roughly 3-4 inches in diameter.
Heat a few inches of oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (175°C).
Fry the empanada dough circles, 2-3 at a time, for 2-3 minutes on each side or until golden brown.
Drain the fried empanada on paper towels and repeat with the remaining dough.
Meanwhile, prepare the filling by sautéing the onion, garlic, and tomato in the same skillet until soft.
Stir the cooked filling mixture into the mashed cannellini beans. Add cumin and paprika and season with salt to taste.
To assemble the empanadas, place a few spoonfuls of the bean filling onto one half of a fried empanada circle.
Brush the edges with a little water and fold the other half of the empanada in half.
Press the edges gently to seal and brush the top with a little more oil to give it a golden glaze.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Serve the empanadas warm, garnished with a drizzle of olive oil if desired.
